_description: Site-wide index of all notation and symbols across all papers in the series.

notation.name: quaternionic wavefunction
notation.meaning: Quaternion obtained by grouping the four real coefficients of a two-component complex spinor into one quaternion.

notation.name: two-component spinor
notation.meaning: Normalized spin-1/2 state written as the column vector (alpha, beta)^T in C^2.

notation.name: spinor components
notation.meaning: Complex amplitudes of a spin-1/2 state vector in C^2.

notation.name: real coefficient coordinates
notation.meaning: Real coefficients obtained by writing alpha = a_0 + a_1 i and beta = b_0 + b_1 i.

notation.name: quaternion algebra
notation.meaning: Hamilton's quaternion algebra with basis 1, i, j, k.

notation.name: quaternion basis units
notation.meaning: Imaginary quaternion units satisfying i^2 = j^2 = k^2 = ijk = -1.

notation.name: two-dimensional complex state space
notation.meaning: Ambient complex vector space for two-component spinors before projectivization.

notation.name: three-sphere
notation.meaning: Unit sphere in four real dimensions; in this paper it appears both as the normalized quaternionic-wavefunction sphere and as the manifold underlying the unit quaternions.

notation.name: quaternion-SU(2) map
notation.meaning: Fixed convention mapping quaternions to 2 x 2 complex matrices so that unit quaternions model SU(2).

notation.name: special unitary group
notation.meaning: Group of 2 x 2 complex unitary matrices with determinant 1, naturally associated with spin-1/2 transformations.

notation.name: unit-quaternion group
notation.meaning: Group of unit quaternions, identified with SU(2) under the fixed map Phi.

notation.name: rotation group in three dimensions
notation.meaning: Group of proper spatial rotations, related to SU(2) by the standard double cover.

notation.name: Pauli matrices
notation.meaning: Standard Pauli matrices generating the spin-1/2 representation of SU(2).

notation.name: normalized state vector
notation.meaning: A normalized representative of a single-qubit pure state in C^2.

notation.name: spinor components
notation.meaning: Complex amplitudes of a single-qubit state vector |ψ⟩ = (α, β)^T.

notation.name: two-dimensional complex vector space
notation.meaning: Ambient complex vector space for single-qubit state vectors before projectivization.

notation.name: three-sphere
notation.meaning: The unit sphere in R^4; in this paper it appears both as the normalized-spinor sphere and as the manifold underlying the unit quaternions.

notation.name: complex projective line
notation.meaning: Projective pure-state space of a single qubit, obtained by quotienting normalized spinors by global phase.

notation.name: Bloch sphere
notation.meaning: The unit 2-sphere used to represent pure single-qubit states after quotienting global phase.

notation.name: Bloch vector
notation.meaning: The unit vector in S^2 associated with a normalized qubit state through the Bloch-sphere map.

notation.name: single-qubit unitary group
notation.meaning: Group of 2 x 2 unitary matrices; this is the full group in which single-qubit gates live before quotienting global phase.

notation.name: special unitary group
notation.meaning: Group of 2 x 2 unitary matrices with determinant 1; used as a representative model for single-qubit gates modulo global phase.

notation.name: single-qubit gate and SU(2) representative
notation.meaning: U denotes a gate in U(2), while V denotes a determinant-1 representative in SU(2) after removing global phase.

notation.name: quaternion algebra
notation.meaning: Hamilton's quaternion algebra over R with basis 1, i, j, k.

notation.name: quaternion or unit-quaternion gate representative
notation.meaning: A quaternion a + b i + c j + d k; when |q| = 1 it models an element of SU(2).

notation.name: quaternion basis units
notation.meaning: Imaginary quaternion units satisfying i^2 = j^2 = k^2 = ijk = -1.

notation.name: unit-quaternion group
notation.meaning: Group of unit quaternions; a Lie-group model of SU(2).

notation.name: chosen quaternion-matrix identification
notation.meaning: The explicit map from H to 2 x 2 complex matrices used in the paper, chosen so that i, j, k align with the x, y, z Pauli axes.

notation.name: Pauli matrices
notation.meaning: Standard Pauli matrices used to express axis-angle rotations in SU(2).

notation.name: unit rotation axis
notation.meaning: Unit vector in S^2 specifying a rotation axis in axis-angle form.

notation.name: rotation angle
notation.meaning: Rotation angle appearing in both the SU(2) and quaternionic axis-angle formulas.

notation.name: double-cover map to SO(3)
notation.meaning: Homomorphism from Sp(1) to SO(3) defined by conjugation on pure quaternions.

notation.name: rotation group in three dimensions
notation.meaning: Group of proper rotations of R^3; the physical Bloch-sphere action factors through this group.

notation.name: canonical single-qubit IR element
notation.meaning: The compiler-facing quaternionic intermediate-representation object storing a canonical unit quaternion for a single-qubit gate action modulo global phase.

notation.name: quaternion coordinates
notation.meaning: The four real coordinates of the quaternion q = w + x i + y j + z k used as the IR payload.

notation.name: unit quaternion gate representative
notation.meaning: A quaternion w + x i + y j + z k; when unit norm it represents an SU(2) element and therefore a single-qubit gate action modulo global phase.

notation.name: single-qubit unitary group
notation.meaning: The full group in which single-qubit gates live before removal of overall phase.

notation.name: special unitary representative group
notation.meaning: The determinant-one unitary group obtained after removing global phase from single-qubit gates.

notation.name: fixed quaternion-SU(2) map
notation.meaning: The explicit map from quaternions to 2 x 2 complex matrices inherited from paper 1, with i, j, k aligned to the Pauli x, y, z generators.

notation.name: unit-quaternion group
notation.meaning: The group of unit quaternions, used as the quaternionic model of SU(2).

notation.name: global-phase equivalence
notation.meaning: Equivalence relation on U(2) defined by multiplication by an overall complex phase.

notation.name: sign equivalence
notation.meaning: Equivalence relation on unit quaternions identifying q and -q as the same single-qubit gate action modulo global phase.

notation.name: normalization map
notation.meaning: Map that divides a nonzero quaternionic quadruple by its Euclidean norm to return to the unit sphere.

notation.name: sign-canonicalization map
notation.meaning: Map choosing one representative from {q, -q} by the rule w > 0, with a first-nonzero-positive tie-break on the equator w = 0.

notation.name: canonical representative map
notation.meaning: Composition of normalization and sign-canonicalization producing the deterministic stored IR representative.

notation.name: canonical hemisphere
notation.meaning: Closed hemisphere of S^3 selected by w > 0 together with the equatorial tie-break used for canonical representatives.

notation.name: axis-rotation families
notation.meaning: Standard single-qubit rotation gates whose quaternion representatives have direct axis-angle form.

notation.symbol: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T
notation.name: named single-qubit gates
notation.latex: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T
notation.meaning: Standard gate names used as frontend syntax examples before translation into canonical quaternion tuples.

notation.name: rotation angle
notation.meaning: Rotation parameter appearing in axis-rotation gates and in the axis-angle interpretation of unit quaternions.

notation.name: single-qubit segment on wire a
notation.meaning: A maximal consecutive run of single-qubit gates on wire a between entangling or other out-of-scope boundaries.
related_symbols: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T

notation.name: canonical single-qubit IR payload
notation.meaning: Canonical quaternionic IR object storing one single-qubit gate action modulo global phase as four real coordinates.

notation.name: unit quaternion representative
notation.meaning: Unit quaternion representing an SU(2) element and therefore a single-qubit gate action modulo global phase under the fixed Phi convention.

notation.name: quaternion coordinates
notation.meaning: Real coordinates of the unit quaternion q = w + x i + y j + z k.

notation.name: fixed quaternion-SU(2) map
notation.meaning: Inherited map from quaternions to 2 x 2 complex matrices under which unit quaternions model SU(2).

notation.name: single-qubit unitary group
notation.meaning: Full group in which single-qubit gates live before removal of overall phase.

notation.name: determinant-one representative group
notation.meaning: Special unitary group used to represent single-qubit gate action after removal of global phase.

notation.name: unit-quaternion group
notation.meaning: Group of unit quaternions, identified with SU(2) under the fixed map Phi.

notation.name: sign equivalence
notation.meaning: Equivalence relation identifying a unit quaternion and its negative as the same single-qubit action modulo global phase.

notation.name: single-qubit segment
notation.meaning: A maximal consecutive run of single-qubit gates on one wire between out-of-scope boundaries.

notation.name: gates in application order
notation.meaning: Ordered list of gates in a single-qubit segment, written in the order in which they are applied to the state.

notation.name: quaternion gate representatives
notation.meaning: Canonical quaternion representatives of the gates g_1,...,g_n after translation into the single-qubit IR.

notation.name: fused quaternion
notation.meaning: Quaternion product of a full single-qubit segment after multiplication in circuit order and canonicalization.

notation.name: fusion operator
notation.meaning: Canonical fused quaternion obtained from the product q_n ... q_1 followed by canonicalization.

notation.name: normalization map
notation.meaning: Map rescaling a nonzero quaternionic tuple back to unit norm before sign-canonicalization.

notation.name: sign-canonicalization map
notation.meaning: Map that chooses one representative from the sign pair {q, -q} using the w > 0 rule with an equatorial tie-break.

notation.name: canonicalization map
notation.meaning: Composition of normalization and sign-canonicalization used to produce the deterministic stored representative.

notation.name: reconstruction operator
notation.meaning: Layered output rule that emits identity elimination, named-gate recognition, axis-aligned rotation recovery, or a generic one-qubit primitive.

notation.name: axis-aligned rotation families
notation.meaning: Single-qubit rotation families recovered exactly when the fused quaternion lies on one coordinate axis in its imaginary part.
related_symbols: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T

notation.symbol: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T
notation.name: recognized named gates
notation.latex: I, X, Y, Z, H, S, T
notation.meaning: Canonical named-gate outputs recognized exactly by the reconstruction table when the fused quaternion matches a stored tuple.

notation.name: segment gate count
notation.meaning: Number of emitted single-qubit gates in a segment before or after optimization.

notation.name: segment-local depth
notation.meaning: Sequential local depth of a single-qubit segment on one wire.

notation.name: syntax-preserving baseline
notation.meaning: Baseline flow that leaves the original single-qubit segment unchanged and performs no fusion.

notation.name: authoritative equivalence check
notation.meaning: Canonical-quaternion equality criterion used to certify that two segments represent the same single-qubit action modulo global phase.

notation.name: unit three-sphere
notation.meaning: Underlying manifold of unit quaternions on which the hemisphere sign-canonicalization rule is defined.

notation.name: rotation angle
notation.meaning: Rotation angle parameter recovered for exact axis-aligned single-qubit outputs.

notation.name: canonical single-qubit IR element
notation.meaning: Canonical quaternionic IR payload for a single-qubit gate action modulo global phase.

notation.name: unit quaternion gate representative
notation.meaning: Unit quaternion representing a determinant-one single-qubit gate action under the fixed quaternion-SU(2) convention.

notation.name: fixed quaternion-SU(2) map
notation.meaning: Map from quaternions to 2 x 2 complex matrices inherited from paper 1.

notation.name: canonical representative map
notation.meaning: Composition of normalization and sign-canonicalization used to choose one deterministic representative from each sign class {q, -q}.

notation.name: normalization map
notation.meaning: Map rescaling a nonzero quaternionic tuple to unit norm before canonical comparison.

notation.name: sign-canonicalization map
notation.meaning: Map choosing one sign representative using the rule w > 0 and an equatorial tie-break when w = 0.

notation.name: sign equivalence
notation.meaning: Equivalence relation identifying q and -q as the same single-qubit gate action modulo global phase.

notation.name: canonical quaternion equivalence
notation.meaning: Authoritative equivalence criterion declaring two segments equivalent when their canonical fused quaternions are equal after normalization and sign-canonicalization.

notation.name: global-phase-aware matrix equivalence
notation.meaning: Interoperable equivalence criterion declaring two single-qubit segment matrices equivalent when they differ only by a global phase.

notation.name: single-qubit segment
notation.meaning: Maximal adjacent run of single-qubit gates on one wire between non-fusible boundaries.

notation.name: segment gate sequence
notation.meaning: Input gate list for a single-qubit segment, written in application order.

notation.name: translated quaternion sequence
notation.meaning: Canonical unit-quaternion representatives of the gates g_1, ..., g_n.

notation.name: canonical fused quaternion of a segment
notation.meaning: Canonical fused quaternion computed from a segment by multiplying translated gate quaternions in circuit order and canonicalizing the product.

notation.name: matrix image of a segment
notation.meaning: 2 x 2 complex matrix obtained by multiplying the SU(2) representatives of the segment gates in application order.

notation.name: phase-aware residual
notation.meaning: Residual norm of U^dagger V after removal of the best-fit global phase, used as a diagnostic for near-equivalence in matrix-based toolchains.

notation.name: process fidelity
notation.meaning: Single-qubit process fidelity diagnostic computed from the phase-aware overlap of two 2 x 2 unitary matrices.

notation.name: trace overlap
notation.meaning: Trace overlap used to estimate best-fit global phase and process fidelity for two single-qubit unitaries.

notation.name: canonical hemisphere
notation.meaning: Closed hemisphere of S^3 selected by the rule w > 0 plus the equatorial tie-break at w = 0.

notation.name: pure analyzer quaternions
notation.meaning: Pure unit quaternions in Im(H) representing analyzer directions a and b in the inherited quaternion-SU(2) convention.

notation.name: analyzer directions
notation.meaning: Unit vectors in S^2 specifying local measurement axes in the Bell/CHSH setup.

notation.name: scalar part of a quaternion
notation.meaning: Real scalar component of a quaternion q = a + b i + c j + d k.

notation.name: fixed quaternion-SU(2) map
notation.meaning: Inherited map from quaternions to 2 x 2 complex matrices under which unit quaternions model SU(2).

notation.name: local spin observables
notation.meaning: Pauli-spin observables a·sigma and b·sigma with eigenvalues plus or minus one.

notation.name: spin projector
notation.meaning: Projector 1/2 (I + s a·sigma) onto the s-eigenspace of spin along analyzer direction a.

notation.name: singlet density matrix
notation.meaning: Density matrix of the two-qubit singlet state written as 1/4(I⊗I - sum_k sigma_k⊗sigma_k).

notation.name: joint measurement probability
notation.meaning: Probability of obtaining outcomes s and t when the singlet state is measured along analyzer directions a and b.

notation.name: Bell correlation function
notation.meaning: Singlet correlation function equal to the expectation of the product of the two local outcomes; in this paper E(a,b) = -a·b = Sc(u_a u_b).

notation.name: CHSH operator
notation.meaning: Operator A(a)⊗(B(b)+B(b')) + A(a')⊗(B(b)-B(b')) used to derive the Tsirelson bound.

notation.name: CHSH value
notation.meaning: Scalar CHSH combination E(a,b) + E(a,b') + E(a',b) - E(a',b').

notation.name: pure-quaternion commutator
notation.meaning: Quaternion commutator of analyzer quaternions; in this paper it equals 2 u_{a×a'}.

notation.name: coherence manifold
notation.meaning: Product manifold S^3 times R_s used as the state-space domain for the v0.1 coherence model.

notation.name: orientation manifold
notation.meaning: Three-sphere identified with SU(2), carrying orientation representation modes.

notation.name: special unitary group
notation.meaning: Lie group identified with S^3 and used to interpret orientation harmonics in representation terms.

notation.name: slice coordinate
notation.meaning: Real-valued slice/scale/spectral-height coordinate in the second factor of M.

notation.name: state Hilbert space
notation.meaning: Hilbert space L^2(S^3 x R) on which the coherence operator acts.

notation.name: governing coherence operator
notation.meaning: Self-adjoint operator -Delta_{S^3} - partial_s^2 + U(s) defining exact and near coherence.

notation.name: Laplace-Beltrami operator on S^3
notation.meaning: Intrinsic Laplace-Beltrami operator on the orientation manifold S^3.

notation.name: slice potential
notation.meaning: Confining potential in the slice coordinate; v0.1 uses the quadratic local model kappa (s-s_*)^2.

notation.name: distinguished slice
notation.meaning: Reference slice around which the quadratic restoring potential is centered.

notation.name: slice stiffness parameter
notation.meaning: Positive parameter controlling curvature of the quadratic slice potential and oscillator spacing.

notation.name: state function
notation.meaning: State in L^2(S^3 x R), generally expanded in orientation-slice eigenmodes.

notation.name: orientation harmonic mode
notation.meaning: Hyperspherical harmonic eigenfunction of -Delta_{S^3} with eigenvalue lambda_ell.

notation.name: slice mode
notation.meaning: Hermite-Gaussian eigenfunction of the one-dimensional quadratic slice operator.

notation.name: separated eigenvalue
notation.meaning: Total eigenvalue lambda_ell plus mu_n for product mode Psi_{ell,alpha,n}.

notation.name: residual coherence functional
notation.meaning: Infimum over E of norm ||A Psi - E Psi||; central coherence residual in the framework.

notation.name: operator coherence score
notation.meaning: Cosine-type alignment score between Psi and A Psi.

notation.name: axis coherence
notation.meaning: Squared normalized projection of Psi onto a distinguished mode Psi_*.

notation.name: family coherence
notation.meaning: Fraction of Psi energy in the family of slice-grounded orientation modes {Psi_{ell,alpha,0}}.
